cards.afterText: Deal 3 damage to Kong. End the turn.
cards.basicText: This card can only be played if you have not maneuvered this turn.
cards.title: eighth wonder of the world

cards.duringText: Your opponent may BOOST their card. Then, you may BOOST this card.
cards.title: Irresistible force

cards.afterText: If you won the combat, you may place the opposing fighter in any space adjacent to Kong. That fighter's movement is set to 0 for their next turn.
cards.title: Smash and grab

cards.afterText: Choose one of the cards that you used to BOOST with and return it to your hand.
cards.title: A king, nay - a god

cards.afterText: Deal 3 damage to the opposing fighter. Then, take 3 damage.
cards.title: reCKLESS LUNGE

cards.afterText: If you won the combat, deal 1 damage to each opposing fighter in Kong's zone.
cards.title: clobber

cards.duringText: For each zone the opposing fighter is in, increase the value of this card by +1.
cards.title: Jaw of the Beast

cards.afterText: Draw 1 card. If you won the combat, draw 2 cards instead.
cards.title: Regroup

cards.afterText: You may move Kong up to the number of spaces equal to the damage received in this combat.
cards.title: pounce

cards.basicText:
Deal 3 damage to Kong.
Gain 2 actions. 
You may also discard a random card to gain 3 actions instead.
cards.title: The King is coming!

cards.basicText: Move Kong up to 5 spaces. You may move Kong through spaces containing opposing fighters.
cards.title: Crash through the Trees

hero.name: King Kong
hero.specialAbility:
King Kong is a large fighter. 

At the start of your turn, you may deal 1 damage to King Kong. If you do, choose an adjacent opponent to discard a card.
hero.quote: The great ape at his terrible scale — a LARGE bruiser who trades his own blood for pressure, rattling cards out of enemy hands just by looming close.

name: King Kong
note:
2.6 = Updated the text on Smash & Grab. (Fighters may still boost their movement).

2.5 = UPDATED ART

Large fighters have an extended base that can occupy up to two spaces. Large fighters may start moving from any space they are in. When they do, rotate them so that the head of the base is moving into the new space. The tail of the base always follows behind the head, entering the space the left. Large fighters also ignore one-way arrows on maps and cannot use secret passages.
Large fighters can attack up to 2 spaces away, even over fighters that occupy one of those spaces.
